metadata.title: Claude, Mixed.
metadata.description: Skill teardown of the explainer (Vox-style mixed-media compositing) pipeline — laundering function, two-axis shot system, slot contract.

beats.narration_text: Hej — this is Liam, in for Bear. The explainer skill is the Vox-style mixed-media compositing pipeline. Not a renderer — a laundering function. Every source, every medium, passes through one treatment and lands on one timeline. This episode is that skill explaining how it unifies what should not cohere.

output: Law: sources never match; the treatment does
Pipeline: plan → audio → run → stills → video → assemble

beats.narration_text: The skill folder carries three reference files that matter. MOTION.md enumerates seven motion languages — hold, kenburns, pan, parallax, isotype, drawon, map — and rules about which enters when. REMOTION.md owns the annotation plane. EQUATIONS.md owns the tangent template. The SKILL.md itself is about ten kilobytes. That compactness is deliberate.

props.title: One treatment. Every source.
props.calloutText: MOTION.md governs which of seven motion languages each beat gets
props.calloutSub: Pantry → media/ → clips/ — the slot contract.

beats.narration_text: Six phases, each gated. Plan first — beats stay under twenty-eight words, shot type locked before source is chosen. Audio second — ElevenLabs records Bear's voice per beat, measures it, and that duration is the master clock from then on. Run assembles whatever exists. Stills fill slots. AI video enters last, per beat, per approval. The annotation plane closes.

props.title: Slots fill last, not first.

phases.label: AUDIO
phases.desc: ElevenLabs mp3 per beat — the clock

phases.label: RUN
phases.desc: Gate A → Manim → Gate B → compile

props.footerNote: Gate 0 = audio lock. Slates hold every unfilled slot until the plate arrives.

beats.narration_text: To demo the plan phase — free, no spend — ask it: what is the Electoral College? The skill reads SKILL.md, detects slate-cut mode, scopes the insight, assigns shot types, and emits a SHOTLIST. That is the output the gate reviews before any audio generates.

props.segment: Run it on a political concept
output: * insight scoped: Electoral College as structural bias
* length tier: MEDIUM (5–12 min) · SHOTLIST ready
* Gate 1: plan approved · audio spend next

beats.narration_text: The SHOTLIST names each beat: the shot type is locked — GRAPHIC, STILL, DOCUMENT — and the source axis is open. Beat one is an isotype grid, count-up, own-production Manim. Beat two is an archive still, Ken Burns toward the faces. Beat three is a comparative map. Beat four is a document scan with annotation. The slot contract means Beat two can be upgraded from still to AI video later without re-editing the timeline.

props.title: SHOTLIST.md — explainer plan output
props.code:
## SHOTLIST — vox-electoral-college

B01  GRAPHIC   own   isotype dot grid — 538 dots
     "538 people choose the president."
     manim: IsotypeDotGrid(count=538, duration=8.2)

B02  STILL     archive  1787 Constitutional Convention
     "The framers were afraid of direct democracy."
     kenburns: focus=[0.5, 0.3] — faces center-left

B03  GRAPHIC   own     state-size vs electoral-weight map
     "Wyoming gets 3 votes. California gets 54."
     manim: IsotypeFraction + StateCardPair

B04  DOCUMENT  archive  NYT 2000 electoral map scan
     "Gore won the popular vote. Bush won 271."
     annotation: ring around Florida, drawon arrow

beats.narration_text: The insight that unlocks mixed media is called the laundering function. You do not design one visual style and match every source to it. Instead, every source passes through a fixed treatment — desaturate, contrast, newsprint ground — and the treatment does the work of unity. Archive, generated, programmatic: it does not matter. They all land the same.

props.heading: Every source lands the same.
props.body: Desaturate eighty percent. Contrast one-point-one-five. Seat on a real newsprint scan. Archive photo, FLUX still, AI clip, Manim fragment — all pass through one treatment and land on one flat annotation plane. The sources never match each other; the treatment makes them match. That is why mixed media reads as one film, and why it is easier than designing a single visual style.
props.quote: Not a style — a laundering function.

beats.narration_text: The two-axis shot system separates what a beat IS from where the media COMES from. The type is the editorial decision — still, footage, document, graphic. That locks at the plan gate. The source — archive, AI-generated, own-production — is a later decision, when you know what you can find and what you can afford. The slot contract makes every upgrade free: same filename, recompile, done.

props.heading: Type is locked. Source is late.
props.body: shot.type is the presentation form — STILL, FOOTAGE, DOCUMENT, GRAPHIC, COMPOSITE, CARD — and it locks at the plan gate. shot.source is provenance — archive, ai, own — and it binds late, after the plate arrives. Collapsing them forces a re-edit every time media swaps. Keeping them separate makes swaps free. A STILL slot upgrades from archive photo to AI video by dropping a new file in media/; the type never changes, the timeline never changes, only the slot recompiles.
props.quote: Collapsing them makes every swap a re-edit; separating them makes swaps free.
props.sparkLine: Type is the argument. Source is the plate.

beats.narration_text: The design tell is the slate. Every unfilled slot renders a charcoal card with the beat id. The film is always watchable, from the first run, with zero plates in media. This signals something about the skill's philosophy: an explainer is not blocked on assets. The SHOTLIST names what to find; the machine fills what it can; the human drops plates in at their own pace. Progress is visible at every stage.

props.heading: The slate is a standard, not a shame.
props.body: When a slot has no media, it renders a charcoal slate: the beat id, a progress note, the brand mark. The compile gate passes. The film is watchable from day one, slates and all. This is the design tell: the pipeline treats a missing plate as a production-standard placeholder, not an error state. The human fills media/ at their own pace; on each re-run only changed slots recompile.
props.quote: The first watchable pass IS the production pipeline running with slates in unfilled slots.
props.sparkLine: Progress is visible at every stage.

beats.narration_text: What the explainer skill actually does: it gives mixed-media compositing a fixed grammar — one treatment for every source, a two-axis system that separates editorial decisions from production logistics, and a slot contract that makes every film watchable from day one. The phases gate spending so the expensive steps enter last, after the argument is already proven to work.

artifactLines: Laundering function: one treatment unifies archive, AI, and programmatic sources.
Two-axis shots: type locked at plan, source bound late — swaps are free.
Slot contract: slates hold unfilled beats; only changed slots recompile.
Six gated phases: audio lock before rendering; expensive steps last.

beats.narration_text: Your turn. Use the explainer skill on any concept you can state as a question. Before you open the terminal, ask: what shot type would make a viewer feel the contrast between what they expect and what is actually true? Name the type before you decide where the media comes from. Then run: ./art explainer, and your concept in quotes.

output: Use the explainer skill on any concept you can state as a question.
Start with: what shot type would make a viewer feel the contrast
between what they expect and what is actually true?
Name the type before you know the source.
